mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signal broad economic strength and robust corporate hiring, leading to upward revisions in aggregate earnings forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the medium term.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between 3-month changes in PAYEMS and 60-day forward returns is consistently below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the year-over-year percentage change in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 3%, it signals persistent inflation that forces the Federal Reserve to adopt a hawkish stance. This increases discount rates and negatively impacts equity valuations.
failure_condition: The S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when the trigger condition is met.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and a rise in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) indicates a severe weakening of consumer health. This joint signal precedes sharp declines in expected corporate revenues, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The dual-trigger signal does not have a statistically significant negative correlation (p > 0.05) with 60-day forward returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In an accommodative monetary policy regime (low FEDFUNDS), a declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor markets and economic growth without triggering fears of imminent policy tightening. This boosts investor sentiment and drives positive short-term equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS is currently very low at 0.08%
failure_condition: The correlation between UNRATE changes and S&P 500 returns is negative or statistically insignificant during periods where FEDFUNDS is below 1%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production (INDPRO) is a direct indicator of output growth in the manufacturing, mining, and utility sectors. This strength in the economy's supply side translates directly into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO shows a rank correlation below 0.03 with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) fall below 1.2 million units on an annualized basis, it signals a severe contraction in the residential construction sector, a key leading indicator of broader economic downturns. This negative signal on a critical component of GDP drives negative equity returns.
failure_condition: In periods following a drop in HOUST below 1200, the average 60-day S&P 500 return is not statistically different from zero (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The level of the federal funds rate is a primary component of the discount rate used for equity valuation. A higher FEDFUNDS rate increases the cost of capital and makes fixed income more attractive relative to stocks, leading to negative pressure on S&P 500 valuations.
failure_condition: The correlation between the FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or insignificant during periods of rising rates.

mechanism_narrative: A positive quarter-over-quarter growth surprise in real GDP (GDPC1) indicates the economy is stronger than previously anticipated. This new information forces analysts to revise earnings estimates upward, causing a rapid, positive repricing of the S&P 500 over a short horizon.
failure_condition: The average 5-day S&P 500 return following a positive GDP growth surprise is not statistically greater than the unconditional average return.
